journal_entry.description: Conversion of bank loan to equity, issuing 400 common shares
journal_entry.rationale: The company is converting a $200 loan payable into equity by issuing 400 common shares. This is a debt-to-equity conversion transaction. The loan liability is eliminated (debit Loan Payable $200) and equity is increased through the issuance of common shares (credit Common Shares $200). The tuples correctly identify this as a liability decrease (debit) and equity increase (credit). No tax implications for this transaction as it is a capital restructuring. Total debits ($200) equal total credits ($200).

output.reason: Converting a bank loan to equity means the liability (loan payable) is being eliminated. Debit side: Liability decrease (1 line). The issuance of common shares affects the credit side only (equity increase).

debit_classifier.reason: Converting a bank loan to equity means the liability (loan payable) is being eliminated. Debit side: Liability decrease (1 line). The issuance of common shares affects the credit side only (equity increase).

output.reason: Converting a loan to equity means the liability (loan payable) is being eliminated and equity (common shares) is being increased. On the credit side: (1) Liability decrease is recorded as a DEBIT (not credit), so the loan payable reduction does NOT appear on the credit side. (2) Equity increase (issuing common shares) = equity increase, which is 1 credit line in slot b.

(2) Equity increase (issuing common shares) = equity increase, which is 1 credit line in slot b.
